frameworks.name: working memory averaging
frameworks.one_line: memory reports reflect reliability-weighted integration across simultaneously held items

frameworks.name: optimal cue integration
frameworks.one_line: multisensory-style reliability weighting extended to unimodal working memory

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: treated as one instance of a broader memory-averaging mechanism

core_question: Do reports of a cued working-memory item get biased by other simultaneously held, relevant items in a reliability-weighted way?
key_assumptions: sensory input is noisy but the world is predictable
integrating multiple observations increases estimate reliability
items are weighted by their signal-to-noise ratio
only attended, task-relevant items enter integration
manipulations: similarity of non-target items to the target orientation
temporal position of items (before vs after target)
key_findings: reports were biased by simultaneously memorized similar, relevant items
bias scaled with item reliability (signal-to-noise weighting)
items both before and after the target biased the report
only attended, task-relevant items produced bias
interpretations: bias arises in working memory, not solely at perception/encoding
serial dependence may be a special case of a general working-memory averaging mechanism
